title: Judge

jokes.text: Oh, Kentucky fried Christ!
jokes.setup: Exclamation of frustration about the heat
jokes.punchline: KFC brand name twisted into profane religious exclamation
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's both profane and specific to the Southern setting, delivered with perfect economy

jokes.text: It's like Satan's humidor out here
jokes.setup: Commenting on oppressive heat and humidity
jokes.punchline: Comparing Alabama weather to devil's cigar storage
jokes.explanation: Vivid metaphor that captures both the heat and Selina's elitist perspective

jokes.text: Hey, Gary, how come your family doesn't have a Mongoloid kid on the porch playing banjo? / 'Cause he grew up and moved to DC.
jokes.setup: Selina making crude stereotype joke about Southern families
jokes.punchline: Gary's self-deprecating response suggesting he IS that kid
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Selina's cruelty met with Gary's unexpected wit and self-awareness

jokes.text: It's like he's never seen his mother before
jokes.setup: Gary's enthusiastic reunion with his mother
jokes.punchline: Selina's dry observation of Gary's overenthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor that reveals Selina's inability to understand normal family affection

jokes.text: That's a real snatch-22
jokes.setup: Describing a no-win situation with donors
jokes.punchline: Catch-22 twisted into crude sexual pun
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay but somewhat predictable for Veep's style

jokes.text: Why am I asking somebody who has sex one and a half times a year to do that?
jokes.setup: Asking Amy to write sexy email rejection
jokes.punchline: Cruel assessment of Amy's sex life with oddly specific fraction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina cruelty with the absurd specificity of 'one and a half times'

jokes.text: It's White City. We couldn't afford Lynch City.
jokes.setup: Selina misidentifying their hometown
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact correction revealing economic status
jokes.explanation: Dry delivery of economic reality with perfect matter-of-fact tone

jokes.text: Stewart's the new Bobby
jokes.setup: Explaining who Stewart is
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Judge's pattern of male companions
jokes.explanation: Sets up running gag about Judge's succession of male 'associates'

jokes.text: Okay, it's a lady
jokes.setup: Confusion about Mr. Marjorie's gender
jokes.punchline: Gary's relieved clarification
jokes.explanation: Simple confusion gag about transgender character's name

jokes.text: No one is gonna read that
jokes.setup: Mike mentioning Selina's memoir
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of book's commercial prospects
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of harsh truth about Selina's relevance

jokes.text: Mike, do you have any awareness of what's been happening with newspapers in the last 10 years? / Not at all. I mean, truth is I don't even get 'em anymore. I just read the news on my phone. It's so much better and it's free.
jokes.setup: Amy's sarcastic question about newspaper industry decline
jokes.punchline: Mike's oblivious confirmation that he's part of the problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike moment - completely missing the irony while proving Amy's point

jokes.text: That's not a word
jokes.setup: Mike using 'dialogued' as a verb
jokes.punchline: Amy's immediate correction
jokes.explanation: Simple grammar correction highlighting Mike's poor writing

jokes.text: It's not
jokes.setup: Mike saying it's okay to eat cello bow rosin
jokes.punchline: Amy's flat contradiction
jokes.explanation: Minimal reaction to Mike's absurd parenting advice

jokes.text: I think we both found that solution very 'rosinable'
jokes.setup: Mike's terrible humor column about daughter eating rosin
jokes.punchline: Forced pun combining 'reasonable' and 'rosin'
jokes.explanation: Deliberately bad dad joke that perfectly captures Mike's terrible writing

jokes.text: I do not want you to do that. / I will. / Don't. / I'm gonna. / No.
jokes.setup: Mike offering to send his terrible writing samples
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate attempts to stop him
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Mike's obliviousness to rejection

jokes.text: What does that mean? No butt-fucking Ned Beatty till the after party, I guess.
jokes.setup: Question about 'New South' party theme
jokes.punchline: Crude reference to Deliverance rape scene
jokes.explanation: Shocking reference that shows Selina's crude humor and cultural knowledge

jokes.text: Yes, who hasn't said the N-word on television
jokes.setup: Describing their choice of Southern chef
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact reference to racist celebrity chefs
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny commentary on celebrity chef scandals delivered with casual tone

jokes.text: Hey, everybody, not home an hour and Gary's carrying a Jimmy Choo purse.
jokes.setup: Judge returning home to see Gary
jokes.punchline: Immediately emasculating Gary in front of guests
jokes.explanation: Establishes Judge's character as immediately hostile and emasculating toward Gary

jokes.text: You'll ruin your mascara
jokes.setup: Gary getting emotional
jokes.punchline: Judge's emasculating comment implying Gary wears makeup
jokes.explanation: Continues Judge's pattern of emasculating Gary with casual cruelty

jokes.text: I did not vote for you, but I do respect the office.
jokes.setup: Meeting the former president
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily stating he didn't vote for her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Southern passive-aggression disguised as politeness

jokes.text: Like Gary's dad
jokes.setup: Discussion of Maryland having slavery both ways
jokes.punchline: Implication that Judge is closeted/bisexual
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double entendre connecting historical and sexual themes with perfect economy

jokes.text: It's not Gary's. It's his brother Bruce's. / Oh, I didn't know you had a brother. / Brue was his stillborn twin.
jokes.setup: Confusion about whose crib is in the room
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation about dead twin brother
jokes.explanation: Shocking dark humor delivered with perfect casual tone, sets up ongoing Bruce references

jokes.text: He had a huge heart. / Missing all its valves, though.
jokes.setup: Sentimental comment about dead baby's big heart
jokes.punchline: Medical reality that contradicts the sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection from sentimental to medical, delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: When Gary was in high school, I used to say Bruce still had a better chance of making the football team.
jokes.setup: Continuing discussion of dead twin Bruce
jokes.punchline: Saying dead baby was more athletic than living Gary
jokes.explanation: Brutally funny callback that perfectly establishes Judge's cruelty toward Gary - comparing him unfavorably to a dead baby

jokes.text: I'll tell you something, if I had a crib for every baby who died inside me, I could open up a Pottery Barn Kids.
jokes.setup: Discussion of keeping dead baby's crib
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to multiple miscarriages as retail opportunity
jokes.explanation: Shocking, perfectly crafted dark humor that transforms personal tragedy into commercial metaphor with trademark Selina callousness

jokes.text: At least while the government's shut down, we won't have to pay taxes. That's not how it works.
jokes.setup: Comment about government shutdown benefits
jokes.punchline: Correction of fundamental misunderstanding of how taxes work
jokes.explanation: Simple ignorance/correction beat that captures TV pundit stupidity.

jokes.text: I got this really cheap flight. Had, like, four layovers. / I really got here three hours early because we had to make an emergency landing in Birmingham because some guy took a poo on the beverage cart.
jokes.setup: Mike explaining his travel difficulties
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd travel story culminating in bathroom incident
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike rambling that escalates to absurd TMI territory

jokes.text: And, no, it wasn't me. I'm actually really blocked up from all the travel.
jokes.setup: Mike's denial about airplane incident
jokes.punchline: Immediate TMI about his constipation
jokes.explanation: Classic Mike oversharing that makes his denial even more uncomfortable

jokes.text: We're actually doing a 'herstorical' tour of great Southern female writers. / And where they killed themselves.
jokes.setup: Catherine's feminist wordplay about historical tour
jokes.punchline: Marjorie's dark addition about suicide sites
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from Catherine's earnest feminism to Marjorie's morbid reality

jokes.text: I tried to explain to him that Birmingham ranks low on the jihadi to-do list.
jokes.setup: Explaining security concerns about Jaffar
jokes.punchline: Calm assessment of Birmingham's terrorist target value
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard deadpan logic applied to terrorism concerns

jokes.text: our family's got a brand-new imam who's just a lot more chill. I mean, I can engage in homosexual acts with the entire writing staff of Charlie Hebdo and nobody would say boo.
jokes.setup: Explaining his new religious freedom
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific example involving French satirical magazine
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and absurd example of religious liberalization

jokes.text: Hey, Dan, look at my sandwich. Remind you of Jane?
jokes.setup: Jonah showing sandwich to Dan
jokes.punchline: Crude comparison to Jane's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Typical juvenile Jonah humor that reveals his obsession with Jane

jokes.text: You're a freshman congressman who still uses his mother's Netflix password.
jokes.setup: Dan dismissing Jonah's political leverage
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about Jonah's immaturity
jokes.explanation: Perfect specific detail that captures Jonah's arrested development

jokes.text: You said on-air that Bruce Hornsby was a member of the Grateful Dead. / He was a touring member from '90 to '92. He never officially joined.
jokes.setup: Dan citing Jonah's on-air mistake
jokes.punchline: Jonah's overly specific correction that misses the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah pedantry that shows he misses the broader point while being technically correct

jokes.text: Jesus Christ, it's not like I'm gonna rape the president. / Oh! / I'm sorry, did you just say you aren't going to rape the president?
jokes.setup: Jonah's inappropriate protest of innocence
jokes.punchline: Montez's horrified response highlighting how wrong it sounds
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe moment where Jonah's attempt to sound innocent makes everything worse

jokes.text: Yeah, but I only have two months left of those Jiffy Jew classes. And after that, it's snip, snip, snip.
jokes.setup: Jonah claiming anti-Semitism
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to circumcision and 'Jiffy Jew' classes
jokes.explanation: Absurd Jonah terminology and casual approach to religious conversion

jokes.text: You know, I'm technically from the South. / Oh, is that right? / Yeah, Maryland had slaves. They just didn't secede. / So, they had it both ways?
jokes.setup: Selina claiming Southern credentials
jokes.punchline: Jaffar's innocent question with sexual double entendre
jokes.explanation: Good historical humor with accidental callback to Judge sexuality theme

jokes.text: I'll show that oil-splatted cow-fucker that I'm open-minded and nonjudgmental.
jokes.setup: Selina planning to impress Quartie with her tolerance
jokes.punchline: Immediately using crude insults to describe him
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - claiming tolerance while using elaborate crude insults

jokes.text: Do you know that if 30,000 more people in Alabama had gone to the polls instead of smoking off-brand cigarettes through their neck holes at riverboat casino slot machines...
jokes.setup: Amy's frustration about the election loss
jokes.punchline: Highly specific and crude stereotype about Alabama voters
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and harsh stereotype delivered with perfect Amy bitterness

jokes.text: It's not urine, okay? It's probably just water from the urinal.
jokes.setup: Mike defending his stained jacket
jokes.punchline: Defense that makes it sound even worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike logic that makes his situation sound worse while defending it

jokes.text: Did you have your pants on? / Of course I did. What are you talking about?
jokes.setup: Gary's innocent hunting story about being knocked unconscious
jokes.punchline: Selina immediately jumping to sexual abuse implications
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Selina's immediately suspicious mind and Gary's innocence

jokes.text: I skinned it and I made a fur vest and matching boots for my G.I. Joe doll.
jokes.setup: Gary's touching story about feeling like a man
jokes.punchline: Revelation he used it for doll fashion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - taking masculine activity and making it about fashion for dolls. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: He had a dead animal in his pocket the whole time? / And you know what I did with it? I skinned it and I made a fur vest and matching boots for my G.I. Joe doll.
jokes.setup: Gary's story about fake hunting success
jokes.punchline: Gary's feminine craft project with the dead squirrel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - taking masculine hunting story and making it about dollmaking

jokes.text: Oh, my goodness, I wish I could say the same thing. We need a drink. I'm seven years sober, Ma'am.
jokes.setup: Quartie saying nice things about President Hughes
jokes.punchline: Selina's backhanded response followed by awkward drink offer to sober person
jokes.explanation: Good escalating awkwardness - insult followed by inappropriate drink offer to recovering alcoholic.

jokes.text: It's only got four wheels and there's no fucking hot tub in the back.
jokes.setup: Jonah complaining about the limo
jokes.punchline: Absurd expectation of hot tub in limousine
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah entitlement with absurd luxury expectations

jokes.text: We're gonna fuck bitches till they in stitches. Actually, I'm just kidding. I got engaged.
jokes.setup: Jonah's crude party plans
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission he's engaged and not actually planning this
jokes.explanation: Shows Jonah's fake bravado immediately deflated by reality

jokes.text: 9-11, bitches! We're gonna fly two planes into the club!
jokes.setup: Jonah's party enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Casually referencing 9/11 as party metaphor
jokes.explanation: Offensive Jonah humor that shows his inappropriate references

jokes.text: We're just uncomfortable around so many Confederate flags. / And the lawn jockeys.
jokes.setup: Guests explaining why they're leaving party
jokes.punchline: Additional racist decoration that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how tone-deaf the party decorations are

jokes.text: So, you know, Gary and I both have our swimming holes and our fishing holes and all the holes you could ever possibly want.
jokes.setup: Selina's fake Southern speech about shared experiences
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about 'holes' with suggestive delivery
jokes.explanation: Decent sexual innuendo delivered during inappropriate speech context

jokes.text: He just wanted to make his little girl as happy as a hound dog with a horse's Johnson.
jokes.setup: Selina's touching story about father-daughter bonding
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual metaphor at emotional climax
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - ruining sentimental story with crude sexual reference

jokes.text: There's no food here, and the baby and I just got so hungry, and I need you to not be mad at me because I just... I just had... I had one bite.
jokes.setup: Marjorie explaining why she's eating at the party
jokes.punchline: Treating normal party food like forbidden temptation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Marjorie's eating disorder/guilt issues

jokes.text: Did your lady boss send you down to get her 'tampoons,' did she? / No, that's not till next week.
jokes.setup: Judge emasculating Gary with tampon reference
jokes.punchline: Gary's matter-of-fact response about menstrual schedule
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment where Gary's honest response makes emasculation worse

jokes.text: I am so sick of you lording over me how much you know about football and hair color and how much better dead baby Bruce is than me!
jokes.setup: Gary finally standing up to his father
jokes.punchline: Including 'dead baby Bruce' in list of father's superior knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and callback - Gary's emotional breakthrough includes absurd reference to being compared to dead sibling.

jokes.text: Just shut up, Daddy! I am so sick of you lording over me how much you know about football and hair color and how much better dead baby Bruce is than me!
jokes.setup: Gary finally standing up to his father
jokes.punchline: Including 'dead baby Bruce' in list of father's expertise areas
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback where Gary's moment of strength still includes reference to being worse than dead baby

jokes.text: I dropped it over the side of the Staten Island Ferry while pretending to be Melanie Griffith in 'Working Girl.'
jokes.setup: Explaining why he doesn't have his phone
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific pop culture reenactment gone wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd specificity that captures Dan's media obsessions

jokes.text: when I was in high school, I used to pretend to be sick to stay home and jerk off to Jane McCabe.
jokes.setup: Jonah explaining his revenge on Dan
jokes.punchline: Unnecessary TMI about teenage masturbation habits
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah oversharing that makes everyone uncomfortable

jokes.text: Wait, this is Leon West's diary. / What? / Oh, my God, it is. / So, where's yours? / And the little boy with the broken roller skates? He grew up to be Egyptian President Anwar Sadat.
jokes.setup: Discovering they have the wrong diary
jokes.punchline: Mike reading absurd fake historical entry
jokes.explanation: Absurd historical fiction that perfectly captures wrong diary's tone

jokes.text: He's pissing himself!
jokes.setup: Watching his plan backfire on TV
jokes.punchline: Jonah's gleeful response to someone else's failure
jokes.explanation: Shows Jonah's petty enjoyment of others' misfortune

jokes.text: It's actually... it's like what my daddy used to say. He used to say, 'If that story was like a horse, it'd be a unicorn. 'Cause it was magical.'
jokes.setup: Gary trying to compliment Selina
jokes.punchline: Stealing Quartie's nonsensical saying word-for-word
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Gary stealing someone else's folksy wisdom

jokes.text: No wonder you had a heart attack when you were 39.
jokes.setup: Seeing Gary's enormous plate of food
jokes.punchline: Connecting portion size to his medical history
jokes.explanation: Good observational insult connecting current behavior to past health problems

tmdb_overview: Selina takes a trip. Meanwhile, Dan woos Jonah in order to get a coveted interview, and Amy convinces Selina to put her in charge of a very disorganized Mike.
insight: Judicial chaos fuels 60 jokes in 43 minutes—Veep's densest episode yet.
